Tensile strength, thermal stability and degree of crosslinking have been found to be greater for PEVA containing lower VA content (18 and 24%) and diminished for PEVA with higher VA content (33 and 40%) due to DH aging", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.92, "confidence": 0.91, "perspective": "Industry", "entities": ["PEVA", "vinyl acetate", "28-33%", "18%", "24%", "tensile strength", "crosslinking"]}, {"statement": "PEVA films with 18, 24, 33, and 40% VA content were cured at 150 C under vacuum and subjected to IEC 61215 accelerated damp heat aging at 85 C and 85% RH for 1000 and 2000 hours.", "direct_quote": "PEVA films containing 18, 24, 33, and 40% VA content with the necessary additives have been fabricated and cured at 150 degrees C under vacuum and subsequently subjected to accelerated damp-heat (85 C and 85% RH) aging according to IEC 61215 standard for 1000 and 2000 h", "fact_category": "methodology", "relevance_score": 0.85, "confidence": 0.94, "perspective": "Regulatory", "entities": ["IEC 61215", "PEVA", "85 C", "85% RH", "1000 hours", "2000 hours", "150 C"]}, {"statement": "PEVA with higher VA content (33 and 40%) degraded significantly after 2000 hours of damp heat aging with significant loss in transmittance and mechanical integrity.", "direct_quote": "PEVAs with higher VA content (33 and 40%) have been degraded significantly after 2000 h of DH aging resulting in significant loss in transmittance and mechanical integrity. inadequate % transmittance and relatively high stiffness after 2000 h of DH aging discourages PEVA18 as an encapsulant", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.87, "confidence": 0.9, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": ["PEVA", "33% VA", "40% VA", "2000 hours", relatively high stiffness after 2000 h of DH aging.", "direct_quote": "the results of this investigation encourage the PV industry to reduce the VA content in PEVA down to 24% for better encapsulation response against DH aging. inadequate % transmittance and relatively high stiffness after 2000 h of DH aging discourages PEVA18 as an encapsulant", "fact_category": "causal_link", "relevance_score": 0.83, "confidence": 0.89, "perspective": "Industry", "entities": ["PEVA", "24% VA", "DH aging", "transmittance", "stiffness"]}], "evidence_summary": "Key study on PEVA encapsulant compositional optimization for damp heat resistance. Lower VA content (24%) shows better tensile strength and crosslinking retention after IEC 61215 DH aging (85C/85%RH, up to 2000h). Higher VA (33-40%) loses mechanical integrity."}, {"source_url": "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10219392/", "source_title": "The Use of Warm Air for Solvent Evaporation in Adhesive Dentistry", "source_type": "journal_article", "source_quality": 0.48, "overall_relevance": 0.1, "year": 2023, "authors": [], "venue": "Journal of Functional Biomaterials", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "Meta-analysis of 27 studies found warm air for solvent evaporation statistically significantly increased bond strength for etch-and-rinse adhesives (p=0.005) and self-etch adhesives.", "direct_quote": "The results of the meta-analysis for etch-and-rinse adhesives revealed that the use of warm air for solvent evaporation was statistically significantly higher (p = 0.005). For self-etch adhesives and silane-based materials, this effect was observed too. A total of 6626 articles were retrieved from all databases. From this, 28 articles were included in the qualitative analysis", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.12, "confidence": 0.85, "perspective": "Methodological", "entities": ["warm air", "solvent evaporation", "bond strength", "meta-analysis", "p = 0.005"]}], "evidence_summary": "Dental adhesion meta-analysis on warm air solvent evaporation effects.
